brief: GAP/RATIONALE: CRT non-response rate reaches ~30% in HF patients with LBBB; need better baseline predictors of who will not respond. Aim: assess whether baseline q waves in leads I, V5, or V6 predict CRT response in HF + LBBB patients. DESIGN/SETTING: single-cohort retrospective enrollment of patients receiving CRT implantation. POPULATION: HF patients with LVEF <=35% and LBBB undergoing CRT. n=132 total; mean age 63.0 +/- 10.4 yrs; 94 (71.2%) male. INTERVENTION/COMPARISON: stratified by baseline ECG q waves in leads I, V5, or V6 -> qLBBB(+) group = 32 patients with such q waves; qLBBB(-) group = remaining 100 patients without. Predictor of interest = presence of any q wave in I/V5/V6. OUTCOME DEFINITION: non-response to CRT = LVEF improvement <5% at 6-month follow-up. KEY RESULTS (exact numbers): CRT non-response rate qLBBB(+) 68.8% vs qLBBB(-) 33.3%, p<.001. Multivariable logistic regression: baseline q waves in I/V5/V6 independently associated with higher CRT non-response, odds ratio 4.8, 95% CI 1.5-15.0, p=.007. TAKEAWAY: any q wave in leads I, V5, or V6 is an independent predictor of CRT non-response in HF + LBBB patients.
